justification: B aligns with GT monolayer classification and correctly identifies material quality indicators while addressing instrumental effects. A misclassifies layer number despite accurate peak math. Both err on substrate, but B is superior overall on correctness to reference and reasoning coherence.

step1.A: Partial: correct bands/no D but wrong on bilayer (vs GT mono) and substrate (Si vs Cu)
step1.B: Mostly correct: right on monolayer graphene, bands, no D; wrong only on substrate

step2.A: Methodical and grounded but reaches incorrect layer conclusion vs GT; no broadening consideration
step2.B: Logical, accounts for resolution effects; minor looseness on '>1' ratio but coherently reaches GT-matching classification

step3.A: Detailed with stacking/purity but over-specifies wrong bilayer; good confidence/peaks
step3.B: Concise, clear key evidence and appropriate confidence; matches target without extra errors

thinking: Both candidates correctly identified G (~1580 cm⁻¹) and 2D (~2670 cm⁻¹) bands, absent D band, and Si substrate peaks (common for transferred CVD graphene). GT specifies monolayer on Cu with I(2D)/I(G)>2 and 2D FWHM 25-35 cm⁻¹. A correctly notes observed ratio ~1.34 and FWHM~40 but wrongly concludes bilayer; B correctly concludes monolayer per GT while considering instrumental broadening from broad Si peak. Both missed Cu growth substrate. B matches classification target better.
